content: The state-of-the-art 2024–2025 active learning agent memory architectures can be reframed as a hierarchical curiosity filtering system, where long-term memory slots are only allocated to exploration experiences that reduce the agent’s prediction error (the core metric of curiosity-driven reinforcement learning), rather than storing all encountered data. This creates a self-organizing memory that prioritizes high-value learning opportunities over redundant or predictable observations, automatically aligning memory growth with the agent’s active learning objective.
